     1→# CONTEXT.md — HarmonyForge (Root)
     2→
     3→*Project-wide context. Every agent operating anywhere in this repository inherits these terms, decisions, patterns, and policies.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Ontology
     8→
     9→| Term | Definition |
    10→|---|---|
    11→| **HarmonyForge** | An ante-hoc "Glass Box" co-creative system for SATB music arrangement |
    12→| **Tactile Sandbox** | The primary editing screen — symbolic score rendering (VexFlow) + playback (Tone.js) |
    13→| **Theory Inspector** | The sidebar UI — LLM-driven Glass Box reasoning and Red Line violation flags |
    14→| **Glass Box** | The design principle: all reasoning is visible and anchored in academic sources, never opaque |
    15→| **Intrinsic Determinism** | Every claim is anchored to an academic definition, not probabilistic folk knowledge |
    16→| **Red Line** | Inline violation annotations on the VexFlow canvas (parallel fifths, voice crossings, etc.) |
    17→| **SATB** | Soprano, Alto, Tenor, Bass — the four-voice arrangement format this system specializes in |
    18→| **Symbolic** | Music represented as pitch/duration/voice data (MusicXML/JSON) — never raw audio or waveforms |
    19→| **Nocturne** | The dark color palette / theme for HarmonyForge |
    20→| **Sonata** | The light color palette / theme for HarmonyForge |
    21→| **Superman** | The human owner of this project; the final authority on all decisions |
    22→| **Ralph Wiggum Loop** | The autonomous execution pattern: loop.sh → PROMPT_build.md → one task → backpressure → commit |
    23→| **Backpressure** | `npx tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` must pass before every commit; failure is a hard stop |
    24→| **FIC** | Frequent Intentional Compaction — the upstream planning workflow (research → plan → implement) |
    25→| **ADR** | Architecture Decision Record — permanent, immutable log of why a technical decision was made |
    26→| **Spec** | JTBD specification document — observable user outcomes with testable acceptance criteria |
    27→| **JTBD** | Job to Be Done — the user outcome framing used inside every spec |
    28→
    29→---
    30→
    31→## Decisions
    32→
    33→| ADR | Decision | Status |
    34→|---|---|---|
    35→| [ADR-001](docs/adr/ADR-001-adopt-ralph-wiggum-loop.md) | Adopt Ralph Wiggum Loop as primary autonomous execution pattern | Accepted |
    36→
    37→All ADRs live in `docs/adr/`. Index: `docs/adr/overview.md`.
    38→
    39→---
    40→
    41→## Patterns
    42→
    43→- **Three Gears**: Specification (Gear 1) → Planning with planner (Gear 2) → Execution via loop (Gear 3)
    44→- **HCI Protocol**: Every interactive session follows preview → gate → execute (`.claude/rules/hci-protocol.md`)
    45→- **Theory Named Strategy**: Every music theory claim is anchored — "X is defined as [definition], per [source]"
    46→- **Architecture Boundary**: Frontend renders/schedules/surfaces; constraint logic lives in the backend only
    47→- **Atomic Design**: UI components are organized as atoms → molecules → organisms
    48→
    49→---
    50→
    51→## Policies
    52→
    53→- **Never run `loop.sh` on `main` or `master`** — feature branches only
    54→- **Never introduce ad-hoc hex color values** — Nocturne/Sonata tokens only
    55→- **Never implement constraint-satisfaction or violation-detection logic in the frontend** — backend only
    56→- **Never edit the rationale, options, or consequences of an Accepted ADR** — supersede instead
    57→- **Always run `npx tsc --noEmit` before committing**
    58→- **Known pre-existing issue:** `npm run lint` fails with `zod/v4/core` module error (Node 25 / eslint-config-next 16.1.6 incompatibility) — use `npx tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` as authoritative backpressure until resolved
    59→
    60→---
    61→
    62→## Research
    63→
    64→| Resource | Purpose |
    65→|---|---|
    66→| `PLAN.md` | 4-phase HarmonyForge redesign roadmap (PRD) |
    67→| `AGENTS.md` | Operational rulebook — backpressure commands, stack constraints, ADR generation rules |
    68→| `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` | Current task list for the loop |
    69→| `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` | JTBD spec for Phase 2–4 |
    70→| `docs/adr/overview.md` | ADR index |
    71→| `docs/context-engineering-guide.md` | Full glossary and workflow reference |
    72→| `docs/notes/prompting-guide.md` | Step-by-step prompt guide for Superman |

    75→| `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` | Stack constraints, design token rules |
    76→| `design-system.md` | Full token spec (colors, typography, spacing, radius, motion) |
    77→

```text
     1→# Context Engineering — Reference Guide
     2→
     3→*The complete reference for the Ralph Wiggum loop workflow: concepts, key terms, purpose, and the mindset shift required to use it effectively.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Key Terms Glossary
     8→
     9→### ADR (Architecture Decision Record)
    10→
    11→A permanent, immutable document that records *why* a technical decision was made — not what was built, but why it was built that way. An ADR captures the options considered, the trade-offs evaluated, and the conditions under which the decision would be reversed. Once accepted, an ADR is never edited. When a decision changes, a new ADR supersedes the old one.
    12→
    13→**Why it matters:** Without ADRs, architectural reasoning lives only in your head and in conversation history that disappears. ADRs give every future agent — and future you — the same context that existed at the moment of decision.
    14→
    15→**Lives in:** `docs/adr/` | **Template:** `docs/adr/template.md` | **Index:** `docs/adr/overview.md`
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→### Spec (Specification)
    20→
    21→A document that defines *what a user should be able to do* when a feature is complete. A spec is written in terms of observable, testable outcomes — not implementation details. Each outcome becomes an acceptance criterion with a pass/fail checkbox.
    22→
    23→**Why it matters:** A spec is the contract between you and the loop. The loop cannot reliably build what hasn't been defined. Vague specs produce vague code. A well-written spec means the loop can work autonomously without asking you what "done" means.
    24→
    25→**Lives in:** `specs/` | **Example:** `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md`
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→### JTBD (Job to Be Done)
    30→
    31→The framing convention used inside each spec. A JTBD describes a complete user outcome: "A composer opens HarmonyForge and can play back an SATB arrangement symbolically." It names the user, the context, and the observable result — no implementation details.
    32→
    33→**Why it matters:** JTBD keeps specs focused on user value rather than technical tasks. It prevents specs from accidentally encoding implementation decisions that belong to the loop.
    34→
    35→---
    36→
    37→### Acceptance Criterion (AC)
    38→
    39→A single, testable condition inside a spec that defines pass/fail for one aspect of a feature. Each AC maps to exactly one checkbox. An AC is only valid if it can be verified without human judgment. If it requires judgment, it belongs in an LLM-as-judge call via `llm-review.ts`.
    40→
    41→**Why it matters:** ACs are the only thing the loop is held accountable to. If an AC is vague, the loop will pass it incorrectly. If an AC is untestable, it cannot be verified programmatically.
    42→
    43→---
    44→
    45→### Backpressure
    46→
    47→The mechanism that prevents the loop from committing broken code. Before every commit, the loop must pass all commands listed in `AGENTS.md` — currently `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run build`. If any command fails, the loop must fix the error before it is permitted to commit. Failure is a hard stop, not a warning.
    48→
    49→**Why it matters:** Backpressure is what separates this workflow from "vibe coding." Without it, the loop can commit plausible-looking but broken code at high speed. With it, every commit is guaranteed to have passed the type-checker and build.
    50→
    51→**Defined in:** `AGENTS.md`
    52→
    53→---
    54→
    55→### Context Window
    56→
    57→The finite working memory available to a Claude model in any single session. Once consumed, earlier content is compressed or dropped. The loop is designed around this constraint: each iteration starts with a fresh, empty context window, fed only the current task via `PROMPT_build.md`. This eliminates context degradation entirely.
    58→
    59→**Why it matters:** Every failure mode of long interactive sessions — task bleed, compounding hallucination, forgetting earlier decisions — is a context window problem. The loop's atomicity (one task, one context, one commit) is the engineering solution.
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→### The Ralph Wiggum Loop
    64→
    65→The autonomous execution pattern at the core of this workflow. A bash script (`loop.sh`) continuously feeds `PROMPT_build.md` to Claude in headless mode. Claude picks one task from `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`, implements it, runs backpressure checks, commits, and exits. The loop immediately restarts with a fresh context for the next task.
    66→
    67→**Why it matters:** The loop converts a prioritized task list into a stream of verified commits with no human involvement required per commit. It is the execution engine. Planning and steering happen outside it.
    68→
    69→**Named after:** The Simpsons character — the loop is deterministic, simple, and repeats exactly what you tell it to. | **Runs via:** `bash loop.sh`
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→### AGENTS.md
    74→
    75→The operational rulebook read by every agent in every loop iteration. It contains the exact backpressure commands, stack constraints, accessibility rules, and ADR generation rules the agent must follow. When you discover a new constraint or fix a broken assumption, you update `AGENTS.md` — the next iteration picks it up automatically.
    76→
    77→**Why it matters:** AGENTS.md is how you steer the loop without interrupting it. It is the persistent memory that survives context resets. Every rule you add here is enforced on every future iteration.
    78→
    79→---
    80→
    81→### IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
    82→
    83→The shared task list between you and the loop. The planning agent writes it; the build agent executes from it. Each task is a single, scoped unit of work with explicit acceptance criteria. The loop picks the top uncompleted task each iteration, marks it done, and moves on.
    84→
    85→**Why it matters:** This file is the state of the current phase. It answers "what's left to build?" at any point. It is also the steering wheel — editing it changes what the loop does next without interrupting a running iteration.
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→### LLM-as-Judge (`llm-review.ts`)
    90→
    91→A typed stub in `src/lib/llm-review.ts` that, when implemented, allows the loop to evaluate subjective acceptance criteria it cannot verify programmatically. It spins up a multimodal model, passes it an artifact (screenshot, text, URL) and a plain-language criterion, and returns a binary pass/fail.
    92→
    93→**Why it matters:** Some ACs — "the score canvas renders all voices with distinct colors and no overlapping noteheads" — cannot be expressed as a type-check. The LLM-as-judge gives those criteria the same hard-stop backpressure as programmatic checks.
    94→
    95→**Currently:** A typed stub. Will be implemented when the first subjective AC is encountered.
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→### FIC (Frequent Intentional Compaction)
   100→
   101→The manual planning workflow already documented in `CLAUDE.md` that pre-dates the loop. FIC uses `/research_codebase` → `/create_plan` → `/implement_plan` to periodically summarize session state into artifact files, preventing context degradation in interactive sessions. FIC is the **upstream planning layer**; the loop is the **downstream execution layer**. They are complementary.
   102→
   103→**Why it matters:** FIC governs what goes into `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`. The loop governs how that plan gets executed.
   104→
   105→---
   106→
   107→### HCI Protocol
   108→
   109→The interactive session protocol defined in `.claude/rules/hci-protocol.md`: **preview → gate → execute**. Every change proposed in a direct Claude session must go through a structured XML preview, receive explicit approval, and only then be executed. The loop operates outside this protocol because it executes within an already-approved plan.
   110→
   111→**Why it matters:** The gate prevents automation bias — the tendency to approve changes without reading them. The loop is authorized at the phase level; individual commits do not require per-commit approval.
   112→
   113→---
   114→
   115→### Supersede
   116→
   117→The correct way to update an ADR. When a decision changes, a new ADR is written that states "Supersedes ADR-00X." The old ADR's status line is changed from `Accepted` to `Superseded by ADR-00Y`. Everything else in the old ADR is frozen permanently.
   118→
   119→**Why it matters:** The immutability of accepted ADRs is what makes them trustworthy. If ADRs could be quietly edited, you could never know whether the recorded reasoning reflected what was actually decided at the time.
   120→
   121→---
   122→
   123→## The Workflow, End to End
   124→
   125→```
   126→You decide what to build
   127→        ↓
   128→Write a spec in specs/           ← Gear 1: Specification
   129→(observable outcomes + ACs)
   130→        ↓
   131→Run the planner                  ← Gear 2: Planning (supervised)
   132→cat PROMPT_plan.md | claude -p
   133→        ↓
   134→Planner asks you questions
   135→You answer, it writes
   136→IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
   137→        ↓
   138→You review the task list
   139→Edit it if needed
   140→        ↓
   141→git checkout -b feature/phase
   142→bash loop.sh                     ← Gear 3: Execution (unattended)
   143→        ↓
   144→Loop: pick task → implement
   145→→ tsc + build → commit → repeat
   146→        ↓
   147→Review: git log --oneline        ← Review
   148→Read diffs, read new ADRs
   149→        ↓
   150→Phase done → write next spec
   151→Repeat from top
   152→```
   153→
   154→---
   155→
   156→## Purpose and Importance of Each File
   157→
   158→| File | Purpose | Importance |
   159→|---|---|---|
   160→| `specs/*.md` | Defines what "done" means | **Critical upstream.** Vague spec = vague code. |
   161→| `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` | Ordered task list for the loop | **The steering wheel.** Edit to change direction. |
   162→| `AGENTS.md` | Rules every agent reads | **Persistent memory.** Survives every context reset. |
   163→| `PROMPT_plan.md` | Planning session instructions | Write once, reuse every phase. |
   164→| `PROMPT_build.md` | Per-iteration instructions | Write once, loop uses it forever. |
   165→| `loop.sh` | Execution script | Run it. Only edit to upgrade the model. |
   166→| `docs/adr/*.md` | Permanent architectural reasoning | **The audit trail.** Answers "why" for all time. |
   167→| `docs/adr/overview.md` | ADR index | Scan to understand every major decision at a glance. |
   168→| `src/lib/llm-review.ts` | Subjective AC evaluator | Implement when first visual or tone AC appears. |
   169→
   170→---
   171→
   172→## The Mindset Shift
   173→
   174→The core reframe: **you no longer write code by talking to Claude. You write context — and Claude writes the code.**
   175→
   176→> "Specs are the new code. ADRs are the new commit messages. Your job is upstream."
   177→
   178→| Old job | New job |
   179→|---|---|
   180→| Describe the task in a message | Write acceptance criteria in `specs/` |
   181→| Review code in the chat window | Review commits and ADRs in git |
   182→| Correct Claude mid-session | Stop the loop, update `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`, restart |
   183→| Hold architectural decisions in your head | Write them as ADRs in `docs/adr/` |
   184→| Re-explain context every session | Context lives in files; files survive resets |
   185→
   186→The session is no longer the unit of work. **The commit is the unit of work.**
   187→
   188→---
   189→
   190→## The Three Gears
   191→
   192→### Gear 1: Specification
   193→
   194→**You write, Claude reads later.**
   195→Write `specs/` files before any code is touched. Focus on observable user outcomes, not implementation. Think like a product manager writing acceptance tests, not a developer describing a function.
   196→
   197→### Gear 2: Planning (supervised)
   198→
   199→**You supervise, Claude analyzes.**
   200→Run `cat PROMPT_plan.md | claude -p`. Claude compares `specs/` against `src/`, asks you questions, writes `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`. You review and approve the task list before the loop runs.
   201→
   202→### Gear 3: Execution (unattended)
   203→
   204→**Claude works, you review output.**
   205→Run `bash loop.sh` on a feature branch. One task per iteration, one commit per task, fresh context every time. Review the git log when you're ready. Never intervene mid-iteration.
   206→
   207→---
   208→
   209→## The Architecture of Control
   210→
   211→```
   212→UPSTREAM (you write)            DOWNSTREAM (you review)
   213→────────────────────            ───────────────────────
   214→specs/                          git log
   215→IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md          docs/adr/
   216→AGENTS.md                       src/ (the code itself)
   217→```
   218→
   219→Upstream changes are safe and cheap. Downstream edits — touching code mid-loop — undermine the audit trail and reintroduce context bleed.
   220→
   221→---
   222→
   223→## What Stays Interactive
   224→
   225→Use a direct Claude session (not the loop) for:
   226→
   227→- Design decisions that require your judgment before a spec exists
   228→- Debugging a specific broken component surgically
   229→- Writing an ADR for a decision made in conversation
   230→- One-off changes faster to do interactively
   231→- Anything where the acceptance criterion is "Superman approves this"
   232→
   233→The HCI protocol (preview → gate → execute) governs all interactive sessions.
   234→
   235→---
   236→
   237→## ADR Lifecycle
   238→
   239→| Stage | Meaning |
   240→|---|---|
   241→| `Proposed` | Draft, not yet binding |
   242→| `Accepted` | In effect, **immutable** |
   243→| `Superseded by ADR-00N` | Replaced; this record is frozen |
   244→| `Deprecated` | No longer relevant, not replaced |
   245→
   246→**Allowed edits on an Accepted ADR:** status line only, typo fixes, adding reference links. Rationale, options, and consequences are immutable.
   247→
   248→---
   249→
   250→## Common Mistakes
   251→
   252→**Mixing gears.** Trying to steer during execution by editing files mid-loop. Stop the loop, edit, restart.
   253→
   254→**Vague specs.** Writing "the canvas should look good." Rewrite: "VexFlow renders all four SATB voices at distinct y-positions with no overlapping noteheads."
   255→
   256→**Patching loop output manually.** Reverting a commit without adding a correction task. The loop will repeat the wrong behavior unless `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` reflects what correct looks like.
   257→
   258→**Forgetting to update AGENTS.md.** If the loop keeps making the same mistake, it means AGENTS.md is missing the rule that prevents it. Update it immediately.
   259→
   260→**Skipping the spec.** Running the planner with vague or missing specs. The planner is only as good as its input.
   261→
   262→---
   263→
   264→## Overnight Run Checklist
   265→
   266→Before `bash loop.sh` unattended:
   267→
   268→- [ ] `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` is populated and reviewed
   269→- [ ] On a feature branch — not `main`
   270→- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes clean
   271→- [ ] ADR-001 revisit criteria are filled in
   272→- [ ] `AGENTS.md` constraints are current
   273→- [ ] You know how to read the results (`git log --oneline`)
   274→
   275→---
   276→
   277→## The One-Sentence Version
   278→
   279→> Write specs upstream, let the loop execute downstream, review commits not conversations.
   280→
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./docs/notes/prompting-guide.md`

```text
     1→# Prompting Guide — HarmonyForge Context Engineering Workflow
     2→
     3→*Step-by-step prompts for your current project stage, when to use the loop vs. chat normally, and practical tips.*
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Where You Are Right Now
     8→
     9→| Phase | Status | What It Means |
    10→|---|---|---|
    11→| Phase 1 (Tokens, fonts, globals.css) | Done | Design system tokens are live |
    12→| Phase 2 (React components, VexFlow, Tone.js) | Ready to plan | No components built yet; specs written |
    13→| Phase 3 (Theory tags, metadata) | Not started | Blocked on Phase 2 |
    14→| Phase 4 (Hover/selected states, audit) | Not started | Blocked on Phase 3 |
    15→
    16→**Your next immediate action:** Run the planner to turn `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` into a concrete `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` task list. Then run the loop on a feature branch.
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## The Decision: Loop vs. Normal Chat
    21→
    22→Use this flowchart before every work session.
    23→
    24→```
    25→Do you know exactly what to build? (spec exists, ACs are written)
    26→├── YES → Is it more than ~4 subtasks?
    27→│         ├── YES → Use the loop (Gear 3)
    28→│         └── NO  → Use normal chat (interactive session)
    29→└── NO  → Do you need to explore/decide first?
    30→          ├── YES → Use normal chat to spec + plan (Gear 1 + 2)
    31→          └── NO  → You're blocked — ask a clarifying question first
    32→```
    33→
    34→**Use the loop when:**
    35→- You have a reviewed `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` with specific tasks
    36→- You're implementing features that don't need your judgment per-commit
    37→- You want unattended overnight progress on a clean feature branch
    38→
    39→**Use normal chat when:**
    40→- Writing a spec (observable outcomes, not implementation)
    41→- Debugging a broken component surgically
    42→- Making an architectural decision that needs an ADR
    43→- One-off changes that are faster done interactively (< 3 subtasks)
    44→- Anything where the acceptance criterion is "Superman approves this"
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Prompts by Stage
    49→
    50→### Stage 0 — Before You Do Anything
    51→
    52→Check where you stand. Paste this into any Claude session:
    53→
    54→```
    55→Read IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md and specs/001-satb-sandbox.md.
    56→Tell me: (1) which tasks are done, (2) which tasks are next,
    57→(3) whether the spec covers the next phase. Do not write any code.
    58→```
    59→
    60→---
    61→
    62→### Stage 1 — Gear 2: Run the Planner (Do This Now)
    63→
    64→You have a spec. You need a task list. Run this in a terminal, not a chat:
    65→
    66→```bash
    67→cat PROMPT_plan.md | claude -p
    68→```
    69→
    70→The planner will:
    71→1. Read `specs/001-satb-sandbox.md` against `src/`
    72→2. Ask you clarifying questions
    73→3. Write `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` with ordered, scoped tasks
    74→
    75→**After it finishes:** Read the task list carefully. Edit it if anything is wrong. Do not run the loop until you've reviewed it.
    76→
    77→If you want to run it inside a chat session instead:
    78→
    79→```
    80→Read PROMPT_plan.md and follow its instructions exactly.
    81→The spec is specs/001-satb-sandbox.md.
    82→Do not write any code — only produce IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md.
    83→```
    84→
    85→---
    86→
    87→### Stage 2 — Gear 3: Run the Loop
    88→
    89→Once `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` is reviewed and you're on a feature branch:
    90→
    91→```bash
    92→git checkout -b feature/phase-2-components
    93→bash loop.sh
    94→```
    95→
    96→The loop runs until you stop it with `Ctrl+C`. Each iteration:
    97→1. Picks the top uncompleted task
    98→2. Implements it
    99→3. Runs `npx tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` (backpressure)
   100→4. Commits with an ADR if needed
   101→5. Exits — restarts immediately for the next task
   102→
   103→**You do not touch anything while the loop runs.**
   104→
   105→Check progress at any time without interrupting:
   106→
   107→```bash
   108→git log --oneline
   109→```
   110→
   111→---
   112→
   113→### Stage 3 — Review the Loop Output
   114→
   115→After the loop stops (or you stop it), review everything:
   116→
   117→```bash
   118→# See what was committed
   119→git log --oneline
   120→
   121→# See any new ADRs written
   122→ls docs/adr/
   123→
   124→# Read a specific diff
   125→git show <commit-hash>
   126→
   127→# Check the spec for what's still open
   128→cat specs/001-satb-sandbox.md
   129→```
   130→
   131→In a chat session, you can also ask:
   132→
   133→```
   134→Read git log --oneline output below and docs/adr/overview.md.
   135→Tell me what was built, what ADRs were added, and what tasks remain in IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md.
   136→[paste git log output here]
   137→```
   138→
   139→---
   140→
   141→### Stage 4 — Correct a Wrong Commit
   142→
   143→If the loop committed something wrong:
   144→
   145→1. Do **not** revert and manually patch
   146→2. Stop the loop
   147→3. Add a correction task to `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`:
   148→
   149→```markdown
   150→## Task N+1: Correct [what was wrong]
   151→- Description: [previous task] produced [wrong behavior]. Fix by [correct approach].
   152→- Acceptance: [observable test that proves the fix]
   153→- AC source: specs/001-satb-sandbox.md > AC-00X
   154→```
   155→
   156→4. Restart the loop. It will fix it on the next iteration.
   157→
   158→---
   159→
   160→### Stage 5 — Write a New Spec (Start of Next Phase)
   161→
   162→When Phase 2 is done and you're starting Phase 3, write a new spec in normal chat:
   163→
   164→```
   165→I need to write specs/002-theory-tags.md for Phase 3.
   166→The user job: a composer opens the score and sees Theory-Named tags
   167→(Strophic, Homophonic, SATB, Common Meter, Hymnody) rendered beneath
   168→the score title with academically rigorous definitions sourced from backend JSON.
   169→
   170→Help me write observable, testable acceptance criteria for this feature.
   171→Do not write any code. Format: same structure as specs/001-satb-sandbox.md.
   172→```
   173→
   174→---
   175→
   176→### Stage 6 — Write an ADR (Architecture Decision)
   177→
   178→When you're about to make a significant architectural choice, write an ADR first:
   179→
   180→```
   181→I need to write an ADR for [the decision].
   182→The options I'm considering are: [option A] vs [option B].
   183→Use the template at docs/adr/template.md.
   184→My HarmonyForge stack is: Next.js App Router, TypeScript strict,
   185→Tailwind CSS (Nocturne/Sonata tokens only), VexFlow, Tone.js, Zustand.
   186→Fill it out completely — no placeholders. Status: Accepted.
   187→```
   188→
   189→---
   190→
   191→### Stage 7 — Surgically Fix a Broken Component (Interactive)
   192→
   193→For bugs or one-off fixes that don't need the loop:
   194→
   195→```
   196→Read src/components/[ComponentName].tsx.
   197→[Describe the bug or constraint violation].
   198→Follow the HCI protocol: preview first, wait for my approval, then execute.
   199→```
   200→
   201→The HCI protocol requires you to reply `Yes`, `Proceed`, or `Approved` before Claude writes any code.
   202→
   203→---
   204→
   205→## Practical Tips
   206→
   207→### Tip 1 — The Loop Is Not Magic; the Spec Is
   208→
   209→The loop executes exactly what the spec defines. If your spec is vague, the loop produces vague code. Time spent sharpening acceptance criteria (`[ ]` checkboxes) saves 10x the time debugging loop output.
   210→
   211→**Bad AC:** "The score canvas should look correct."
   212→
   213→**Good AC:** "VexFlow renders all four SATB voices at distinct y-positions with no overlapping noteheads. `role=\"img\"` and `aria-label` are present on the canvas wrapper."
   214→
   215→---
   216→
   217→### Tip 2 — Never Run the Loop on `main`
   218→
   219→`loop.sh` exits with an error if you're on `main` or `master`. This is intentional. Always:
   220→
   221→```bash
   222→git checkout -b feature/[phase-name]
   223→```
   224→
   225→---
   226→
   227→### Tip 3 — When to Chat vs. When to Loop (Quick Reference)
   228→
   229→| Task | Use |
   230→|---|---|
   231→| Debugging a specific component | Normal chat |
   232→| Exploring what exists in the codebase | Normal chat |
   233→| Writing a spec | Normal chat |
   234→| Running the planner | Terminal or normal chat |
   235→| Implementing 1–2 small things quickly | Normal chat |
   236→| Implementing a full phase (10+ tasks) | Loop |
   237→| Making an architectural decision | Normal chat → write ADR |
   238→| Reviewing loop output | Normal chat or `git log` |
   239→| Adding an urgent correction mid-phase | Stop loop → update plan → restart loop |
   240→
   241→---
   242→
   243→### Tip 4 — Keep `AGENTS.md` Current
   244→
   245→If the loop keeps making the same mistake, that means `AGENTS.md` is missing a rule. Add it immediately after you notice the pattern:
   246→
   247→```
   248→Read AGENTS.md. Add the following rule under the appropriate section:
   249→"[specific constraint the agent keeps violating]."
   250→Do not change anything else.
   251→```
   252→
   253→---
   254→
   255→### Tip 5 — Use Normal Chat for ADRs After Decisions
   256→
   257→Every significant architectural choice you make interactively should become an ADR immediately — before you forget the reasoning:
   258→
   259→```
   260→We just decided [X] instead of [Y] because [Z].
   261→Write ADR-00N for this decision using docs/adr/template.md.
   262→Status: Accepted. Date: [today].
   263→Update docs/adr/overview.md to add the new entry.
   264→```
   265→
   266→---
   267→
   268→### Tip 6 — How to Update an ADR
   269→
   270→**Never edit an accepted ADR's rationale, options, or consequences.**
   271→
   272→To change a decision:
   273→
   274→```
   275→ADR-00N is now outdated because [reason].
   276→Write a new ADR-00M that supersedes it.
   277→In the new ADR, add "Supersedes ADR-00N" to the header.
   278→In ADR-00N, change the status line to "Superseded by ADR-00M."
   279→That is the only edit allowed in ADR-00N.
   280→```
   281→
   282→---
   283→
   284→### Tip 7 — What "Done" Actually Means
   285→
   286→A task is done when:
   287→1. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes (no type errors)
   288→2. `npm run build` passes (no compilation errors)
   289→3. The acceptance criteria checkboxes in `specs/*.md` are provably satisfied
   290→
   291→If you're not sure whether something is done, check the spec — not your memory of the conversation.
   292→
   293→---
   294→
   295→### Tip 8 — The Loop Stops at a Broken Build. That Is Correct.
   296→
   297→If the loop stops with a build error, it worked as designed. The loop will not commit broken code.
   298→
   299→To resume after a build failure:
   300→1. Read the error (`npx tsc --noEmit`)
   301→2. Fix it in normal chat (interactively, small scope)
   302→3. Restart the loop
   303→
   304→---
   305→
   306→## Copy-Paste Quick Reference
   307→
   308→### Check current state
   309→```
   310→Read IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. What tasks are done, in progress, and remaining?
   311→```
   312→
   313→### Start the planner
   314→```bash
   315→cat PROMPT_plan.md | claude -p
   316→```
   317→
   318→### Start the loop on a feature branch
   319→```bash
   320→git checkout -b feature/phase-2
   321→bash loop.sh
   322→```
   323→
   324→### Check loop progress
   325→```bash
   326→git log --oneline
   327→```
   328→
   329→### Add a correction task
   330→```
   331→Add this task to IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md after the current task:
   332→"Task N: Correct [issue]. [Description of correct behavior.] AC: [testable criterion]."
   333→```
   334→
   335→### Write a new spec
   336→```
   337→Help me write specs/00N-[feature].md using the same structure as specs/001-satb-sandbox.md.
   338→Focus on observable user outcomes and testable acceptance criteria only. No implementation details.
   339→```
   340→
   341→### Write an ADR
   342→```
   343→Write ADR-00N for this decision using docs/adr/template.md.
   344→Decision: [X]. Options considered: [A, B, C]. Chosen: [X]. Status: Accepted.
   345→Update docs/adr/overview.md to include the new entry.
   346→```
   347→
   348→### Fix a constraint violation interactively
   349→```
   350→Read [file path]. The constraint violation is [describe it].
   351→Preview the fix (HCI protocol). Wait for my approval before executing.
   352→```
   353→
```
</details></tool-use>

---

## (A) How to ensure CONTEXT.md is updated every time

Three enforcement layers, in order of reliability:

**1. AGENTS.md rule (loop-side — strongest)**
Add a mandatory step to the loop's task completion checklist:
> After implementing any task that changes files in a directory, update the `CONTEXT.md` in that directory to reflect what changed. If no CONTEXT.md exists, create it.

The loop reads AGENTS.md on every iteration. This makes CONTEXT.md updates as mandatory as `npx tsc --noEmit`.

**2. PROMPT_build.md step (loop-side — reinforcement)**
Add a checklist item: `[ ] Updated CONTEXT.md for every directory touched`.

**3. Claude Code hook (interactive-session-side)**
Add a `PostToolUse` hook in `.claude/settings.json` that fires after file edits, running a lightweight script that checks whether the edited file's parent directory has a CONTEXT.md and surfaces a reminder if not. This covers interactive chat sessions where the loop isn't running.

**Summary:** Rule in AGENTS.md is the gate; PROMPT_build.md is the reminder; the hook catches interactive drift. All three together make it hard to forget.

---

**context-engineering-guide.md** — add:

- Row in the file purpose table
- Note in Common Mistakes: "Not updating CONTEXT.md after a task"

     1→# HarmonyForge — Agent Operational Guide
     2→
     3→This is the heart of the Ralph Wiggum loop (ADR-001). Every agent running `loop.sh`
     4→must follow these rules exactly before marking any task complete or committing.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Build & Validation Commands
     9→
    10→```bash
    11→# Type-check (run first — fast feedback)
    12→npx tsc --noEmit
    13→
    14→# Lint
    15→npm run lint
    16→
    17→# Build (full Next.js production build — catches bundling errors)
    18→npm run build
    19→```
    20→
    21→> **Note:** There is currently no `npm run test` script. The backpressure commands are
    22→> `tsc --noEmit` + `npm run lint` + `npm run build`. When a test runner is added
    23→> (Vitest is recommended for Next.js 16 + TypeScript strict), update this file and ADR-001.
    24→>
    25→> **Known pre-existing issue (2026-03-09):** `npm run lint` currently fails with
    26→> `Cannot find module 'zod/v4/core'` — a Node 25 / eslint-config-next 16.1.6 incompatibility
    27→> in `zod-validation-error`. This is not caused by any project source file. Use
    28→> `npx tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build` as the authoritative backpressure until resolved.
    29→> Track fix: update `eslint-config-next` when a compatible release ships.
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Backpressure Rules (MANDATORY)
    34→
    35→1. After modifying **any** source file under `src/`, run all three commands above in order.
    36→2. If `tsc --noEmit` fails: fix all type errors before proceeding. Do not suppress with `// @ts-ignore` or `any`.
    37→3. If `npm run lint` fails: fix all ESLint violations before proceeding. Do not add `eslint-disable` comments.
    38→4. If `npm run build` fails: fix all build errors before proceeding. Do not mark the task complete.
    39→5. Do not commit code that does not pass all three commands. A partial output is a broken output.
    40→
    41→---
    42→
    43→## Stack Constraints (enforced per turn)
    44→
    45→Consult `.claude/rules/typescript-patterns.md` before writing any code. Key constraints:
    46→
    47→- **No `any` types.** Define explicit interfaces for all props and state.
    48→- **Tailwind CSS only.** Use Nocturne/Sonata design tokens — never ad-hoc hex values.
    49→- **VexFlow DOM manipulation inside `useEffect` only.** Never manipulate DOM outside a hook.
    50→- **Tone.js symbolic events only.** No `AudioContext`, no raw buffer manipulation.
    51→- **POUR accessibility.** `aria-label`, keyboard navigation, `aria-describedby` on all interactive elements.
    52→- **Constraint logic lives in the backend.** Never compute SATB violations in the frontend.
    53→
    54→---
    55→
    56→## Architecture Decision Record (ADR) Rules
    57→
    58→When implementing a feature that involves a material architectural decision, do the following immediately after implementation — while context is fresh:
    59→
    60→1. Draft a new ADR using `docs/adr/template.md` as the base.
    61→2. Save it as `docs/adr/ADR-NNN-verb-object-scope.md` (next sequential number).
    62→3. Add a row to `docs/adr/overview.md` with Status, Date, Owners, and link.
    63→4. The ADR must contain at least 2 options considered and one unambiguous "We will …" decision sentence.
    64→
    65→If the decision is minor (a utility function, a naming convention), use the Mini-ADR format
    66→(Context / Decision / Consequences / Revisit / References) — still in `docs/adr/`.
    67→
    68→---
    69→
    70→## Loop Hygiene
    71→
    72→- Pick **exactly one task** from `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` per iteration.
    73→- Do not start a second task in the same iteration, even if the first task was trivially fast.
    74→- After completing a task: mark it done in `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`, run all backpressure commands, then commit.
    75→- Commit message format: `feat: <task description>` (or `fix:`, `refactor:`, `chore:` as appropriate).
    76→- If you discover an operational learning (a new constraint, a broken assumption, a better command), update this file immediately and commit the update before the task commit.
    77→
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/specstory-to-context.sh
#
# Reads .specstory/history/*.md, uses claude -p to extract key decisions and
# file changes from each unprocessed session, then appends structured insights
# to the relevant CONTEXT.md files.
#
# Idempotent: processed session IDs are recorded in .specstory/.processed.
# Re-running never duplicates entries.
#
# Usage:
#   bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh              # process all new sessions
#   bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh --dry-run    # preview without writing
#   bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh --since 2026-03-01  # only sessions after date
#
# Requirements:
#   - claude CLI available in PATH (same binary used by loop.sh)
#   - python3 available in PATH
#   - .specstory/statistics.json exists

set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
SPECSTORY_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.specstory"
HISTORY_DIR="$SPECSTORY_DIR/history"
PROCESSED_FILE="$SPECSTORY_DIR/.processed"
STATS_FILE="$SPECSTORY_DIR/statistics.json"

# --- Parse flags ---
DRY_RUN=false
SINCE_DATE=""

for arg in "$@"; do
  case "$arg" in
    --dry-run) DRY_RUN=true ;;
    --since) shift; SINCE_DATE="${1:-}" ;;
    --since=*) SINCE_DATE="${arg#--since=}" ;;
  esac
done

# --- Guards ---
if [ ! -f "$STATS_FILE" ]; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] ERROR: $STATS_FILE not found. Is this a SpecStory project?"
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] ERROR: 'claude' CLI not found in PATH."
  echo "  Install: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v python3 &> /dev/null; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] ERROR: python3 not found in PATH."
  exit 1
fi

# --- Init processed tracking file ---
touch "$PROCESSED_FILE"

echo "[specstory-to-context] Reading sessions from statistics.json..."

# Extract all session IDs and their start timestamps
SESSION_DATA=$(python3 - <<'PYEOF'
import json, sys, os

stats_file = os.environ.get("STATS_FILE")
since_date = os.environ.get("SINCE_DATE", "")

with open(stats_file) as f:
    data = json.load(f)

sessions = data.get("sessions", {})
results = []
for sid, info in sessions.items():
    ts = info.get("start_timestamp", "")
    msg_count = info.get("user_message_count", 0) + info.get("agent_message_count", 0)
    size = info.get("markdown_size_bytes", 0)
    results.append(f"{sid}\t{ts}\t{msg_count}\t{size}")

# Sort by start_timestamp ascending (process oldest first)
results.sort(key=lambda x: x.split("\t")[1])
for r in results:
    print(r)
PYEOF
)

STATS_FILE="$STATS_FILE" SINCE_DATE="$SINCE_DATE" SESSION_DATA_PROCESSED=$(python3 - <<PYEOF
import os, sys

since_date = os.environ.get("SINCE_DATE", "")
lines = """$SESSION_DATA""".strip().split("\n") if """$SESSION_DATA""".strip() else []

for line in lines:
    if not line.strip():
        continue
    parts = line.split("\t")
    if len(parts) < 4:
        continue
    sid, ts, msg_count, size = parts[0], parts[1], parts[2], parts[3]
    if since_date and ts < since_date:
        continue
    if int(msg_count) < 2:
        continue
    if int(size) < 5000:
        continue
    print(line)
PYEOF
)

if [ -z "$SESSION_DATA_PROCESSED" ]; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] No eligible sessions found."
  exit 0
fi

NEW_COUNT=0
SKIP_COUNT=0
ERROR_COUNT=0

while IFS=$'\t' read -r SESSION_ID SESSION_TS MSG_COUNT SIZE_BYTES; do
  [ -z "$SESSION_ID" ] && continue

  # Skip already processed
  if grep -qF "$SESSION_ID" "$PROCESSED_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    SKIP_COUNT=$((SKIP_COUNT + 1))
    continue
  fi

  # Find the history file for this session
  HISTORY_FILE=$(grep -rl "$SESSION_ID" "$HISTORY_DIR" 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)

  if [ -z "$HISTORY_FILE" ]; then
    echo "  [skip] $SESSION_ID — no history file found"
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    continue
  fi

  SESSION_DATE=$(echo "$SESSION_TS" | cut -c1-10)
  SESSION_BASENAME=$(basename "$HISTORY_FILE")

  echo ""
  echo "  [processing] $SESSION_BASENAME ($SIZE_BYTES bytes, $MSG_COUNT messages)"

  # --- Run claude -p to extract structured summary ---
  SUMMARIZE_PROMPT="You are a context distiller for the HarmonyForge project (Next.js, TypeScript, VexFlow, Tone.js, Zustand).

Read this Claude Code session transcript. Extract a JSON summary with EXACTLY this shape — no prose before or after, valid JSON only:

{
  \"session_slug\": \"<3-5 word lowercase-hyphenated description of the session's main work>\",
  \"directories_touched\": [\"<relative paths like src/components, docs/adr, src/store — infer from context>\"],
  \"insights\": [
    \"<one concrete decision, constraint, or pattern discovered — max 1 sentence each>\"
  ]
}

Rules:
- Maximum 5 insights. Each must be specific and actionable (not vague like 'worked on UI').
- Only include architectural decisions, new constraints, patterns, or permanent changes.
- Skip conversational turns, debug attempts, and transient notes.
- directories_touched: list only directories that had files created or modified.
- If you cannot determine directories, use [\".\"]. Never leave the array empty.
- Return valid JSON only.

Session transcript (first 60KB):
$(head -c 61440 "$HISTORY_FILE")"

  SUMMARY=$(echo "$SUMMARIZE_PROMPT" | claude -p 2>/dev/null || true)

  if [ -z "$SUMMARY" ]; then
    echo "  [warn] claude -p returned no output for $SESSION_ID — marking processed, skipping."
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    continue
  fi

  # Parse JSON summary
  PARSE_RESULT=$(python3 - <<PYEOF2 || true
import json, sys

raw = """$SUMMARY"""
# Strip markdown code fences if claude wrapped output
raw = raw.strip()
if raw.startswith("\`\`\`"):
    lines = raw.split("\n")
    raw = "\n".join(lines[1:-1] if lines[-1] == "\`\`\`" else lines[1:])

try:
    d = json.loads(raw)
    slug = d.get("session_slug", "unknown-session")
    dirs = d.get("directories_touched", ["."])
    insights = d.get("insights", [])
    # Print slug
    print("SLUG:" + slug)
    # Print dirs (one per line, prefixed)
    for dr in dirs:
        print("DIR:" + dr.strip("/"))
    # Print insights (one per line, prefixed)
    for ins in insights:
        print("INS:" + ins.replace("|", "-").replace("\n", " "))
except Exception as e:
    print("ERROR:" + str(e))
PYEOF2
)

  if echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep -q "^ERROR:"; then
    echo "  [warn] JSON parse failed — $(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep '^ERROR:' | head -1)"
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    continue
  fi

  SESSION_SLUG=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^SLUG:" | head -1 | sed 's/^SLUG://')
  DIRS=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^DIR:" | sed 's/^DIR://')
  INSIGHTS_RAW=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^INS:" | sed 's/^INS://')

  # Collapse insights into a single pipe-table-safe cell
  INSIGHTS_CELL=$(echo "$INSIGHTS_RAW" | awk 'NR==1{line=$0} NR>1{line=line " · " $0} END{print line}')

  if [ -z "$INSIGHTS_CELL" ]; then
    INSIGHTS_CELL="Session processed — no distinct insights extracted."
  fi

  echo "  Slug: $SESSION_SLUG"
  echo "  Directories: $(echo "$DIRS" | tr '\n' ' ')"

  # --- Route insights to the correct CONTEXT.md files ---

  # Build list of CONTEXT.md targets from touched directories
  CONTEXT_TARGETS=""

  while IFS= read -r DIR_PATH; do
    [ -z "$DIR_PATH" ] && continue

    # Normalise: strip leading ./
    DIR_PATH="${DIR_PATH#./}"

    # Walk from the touched directory up to root, looking for the nearest CONTEXT.md
    CHECK="$REPO_ROOT/$DIR_PATH"
    FOUND=""
    while [ "$CHECK" != "/" ]; do
      if [ -f "$CHECK/CONTEXT.md" ]; then
        FOUND="${CHECK#$REPO_ROOT/}"
        # Normalise root
        [ "$FOUND" = "$REPO_ROOT" ] && FOUND="."
        break
      fi
      CHECK="$(dirname "$CHECK")"
      # Stop at repo root
      if [ "$CHECK" = "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
        [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/CONTEXT.md" ] && FOUND="."
        break
      fi
    done

    [ -n "$FOUND" ] && CONTEXT_TARGETS="$CONTEXT_TARGETS"$'\n'"$FOUND"
  done <<< "$DIRS"

  # Always include root CONTEXT.md
  [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/CONTEXT.md" ] && CONTEXT_TARGETS="$CONTEXT_TARGETS"$'\n'"."

  # Deduplicate
  CONTEXT_TARGETS=$(echo "$CONTEXT_TARGETS" | sort -u | grep -v '^$')

  if [ -z "$CONTEXT_TARGETS" ]; then
    echo "  [warn] No CONTEXT.md targets resolved. Skipping."
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    continue
  fi

  # --- Append to each target CONTEXT.md ---
  WRITE_COUNT=0

  while IFS= read -r CTX_REL; do
    [ -z "$CTX_REL" ] && continue

    if [ "$CTX_REL" = "." ]; then
      CTX_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/CONTEXT.md"
    else
      CTX_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/$CTX_REL/CONTEXT.md"
    fi

    [ ! -f "$CTX_FILE" ] && continue

    # Idempotency: skip if session ID already present in this file
    if grep -qF "$SESSION_ID" "$CTX_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "  [skip] $CTX_REL/CONTEXT.md — session already recorded"
      continue
    fi

    if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
      echo "  [dry-run] Would append to $CTX_REL/CONTEXT.md: $SESSION_DATE | $SESSION_SLUG"
      WRITE_COUNT=$((WRITE_COUNT + 1))
      continue
    fi

    # Ensure the Session Insights section exists
    if ! grep -q "^## Session Insights" "$CTX_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
      printf '\n---\n\n## Session Insights\n\n*Auto-populated by `scripts/specstory-to-context.sh` from `.specstory/history/`.*\n\n| Date | Session | Insights | Source |\n|---|---|---|---|\n' >> "$CTX_FILE"
    fi

    # Append the row (session ID embedded as HTML comment for idempotency check)
    printf '| %s | %s | %s | [↗](.specstory/history/%s) | <!-- %s -->\n' \
      "$SESSION_DATE" \
      "$SESSION_SLUG" \
      "$INSIGHTS_CELL" \
      "$SESSION_BASENAME" \
      "$SESSION_ID" >> "$CTX_FILE"

    echo "  [wrote] $CTX_REL/CONTEXT.md"
    WRITE_COUNT=$((WRITE_COUNT + 1))
  done <<< "$CONTEXT_TARGETS"

  # Mark session as processed
  echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
  NEW_COUNT=$((NEW_COUNT + 1))

done <<< "$SESSION_DATA_PROCESSED"

echo ""
echo "[specstory-to-context] Complete."
echo "  New sessions processed : $NEW_COUNT"
echo "  Already processed      : $SKIP_COUNT"
echo "  Errors / skipped       : $ERROR_COUNT"
if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
  echo "  (dry-run — no files written)"
fi

```

stats_file = os.environ.get("STATS_FILE")

But the issue is that `STATS_FILE` is defined in the shell script, but the heredoc using `'PYEOF'` (with quotes) means the heredoc is literal, so `$STATS_FILE` wouldn't be interpolated. But I'm using `os.environ.get("STATS_FILE")` which should work... unless the environment variable isn't exported.
 
Ah, I see the real problem now — `STATS_FILE` is just a shell variable but it's never exported, so when Python tries to read it from the environment, it gets `None`. I need to either export the variable before passing it to Python or pass it explicitly as an environment variable to the subprocess.

Since I'm using single-quoted heredocs to prevent shell interpolation, the cleanest approach is to pass the values directly as environment variables when invoking Python, or switch to double-quoted heredocs so the shell variables get interpolated into the Python code itself. <tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/specstory-to-context.sh
#
# Reads .specstory/history/*.md, uses claude -p to extract key decisions and
# file changes from each unprocessed session, then appends structured insights
# to the relevant CONTEXT.md files.
#
# Idempotent: processed session IDs are recorded in .specstory/.processed.
# Re-running never duplicates entries.
#
# Usage:
#   bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh              # process all new sessions
#   bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh --dry-run    # preview without writing
#   bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh --since 2026-03-01  # only sessions after date
#
# Requirements:
#   - claude CLI available in PATH (same binary used by loop.sh)
#   - python3 available in PATH
#   - .specstory/statistics.json exists

set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
SPECSTORY_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.specstory"
HISTORY_DIR="$SPECSTORY_DIR/history"
PROCESSED_FILE="$SPECSTORY_DIR/.processed"
STATS_FILE="$SPECSTORY_DIR/statistics.json"

# --- Parse flags ---
DRY_RUN=false
SINCE_DATE=""

for arg in "$@"; do
  case "$arg" in
    --dry-run) DRY_RUN=true ;;
    --since=*) SINCE_DATE="${arg#--since=}" ;;
    --since)   shift; SINCE_DATE="${1:-}" ;;
  esac
done

# --- Guards ---
if [ ! -f "$STATS_FILE" ]; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] ERROR: $STATS_FILE not found."
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] ERROR: 'claude' CLI not found in PATH."
  exit 1
fi

if ! command -v python3 &> /dev/null; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] ERROR: python3 not found in PATH."
  exit 1
fi

# --- Init processed tracking file ---
touch "$PROCESSED_FILE"

echo "[specstory-to-context] Reading sessions from $STATS_FILE..."

# Extract eligible session IDs — pass path as argv[1], since_date as argv[2]
SESSION_DATA=$(python3 - "$STATS_FILE" "$SINCE_DATE" <<'PYEOF'
import json, sys

stats_file = sys.argv[1]
since_date = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else ""

with open(stats_file) as f:
    data = json.load(f)

sessions = data.get("sessions", {})
results = []
for sid, info in sessions.items():
    ts = info.get("start_timestamp", "")
    msg_count = info.get("user_message_count", 0) + info.get("agent_message_count", 0)
    size = info.get("markdown_size_bytes", 0)
    # Filter: must have messages and be above minimum size (5KB)
    if msg_count < 2:
        continue
    if size < 5000:
        continue
    # Filter by since_date if provided
    if since_date and ts < since_date:
        continue
    results.append(f"{sid}\t{ts}\t{msg_count}\t{size}")

# Sort by timestamp ascending (process oldest first)
results.sort(key=lambda x: x.split("\t")[1])
for r in results:
    print(r)
PYEOF
)

if [ -z "$SESSION_DATA" ]; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] No eligible sessions found."
  exit 0
fi

NEW_COUNT=0
SKIP_COUNT=0
ERROR_COUNT=0

while IFS=$'\t' read -r SESSION_ID SESSION_TS MSG_COUNT SIZE_BYTES; do
  [ -z "$SESSION_ID" ] && continue

  # Skip already processed
  if grep -qF "$SESSION_ID" "$PROCESSED_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    SKIP_COUNT=$((SKIP_COUNT + 1))
    continue
  fi

  # Find the history file for this session
  HISTORY_FILE=$(grep -rl "$SESSION_ID" "$HISTORY_DIR" 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)

  if [ -z "$HISTORY_FILE" ]; then
    echo "  [skip] $SESSION_ID — no history file found"
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    continue
  fi

  SESSION_DATE=$(echo "$SESSION_TS" | cut -c1-10)
  SESSION_BASENAME=$(basename "$HISTORY_FILE")

  echo ""
  echo "  [processing] $SESSION_BASENAME  ($SIZE_BYTES bytes, $MSG_COUNT messages)"

  # Truncate history to 60KB for the prompt (stays within context limits)
  TRANSCRIPT=$(head -c 61440 "$HISTORY_FILE")

  # Run claude -p to extract structured summary
  SUMMARY=$(printf '%s\n\n---SESSION TRANSCRIPT---\n%s' \
'You are a context distiller for the HarmonyForge project (Next.js, TypeScript, VexFlow, Tone.js, Zustand).

Read the session transcript below. Return a JSON object with EXACTLY this shape — valid JSON only, no prose before or after, no markdown fences:

{
  "session_slug": "<3-5 word lowercase-hyphenated description of the main work done>",
  "directories_touched": ["<relative path like src/components or docs/adr — infer from filenames mentioned>"],
  "insights": [
    "<one concrete decision, constraint, or pattern — max 1 sentence each>"
  ]
}

Rules:
- Maximum 5 insights. Each must be specific (not "worked on UI").
- Only include architectural decisions, new constraints, confirmed patterns, or permanent file changes.
- Skip debug attempts, exploratory conversation, and transient notes.
- directories_touched: list directories that had files created or modified. Use "." for root-level files.
- If you cannot determine specific directories, use ["."].
- Return valid JSON only.' \
    "$TRANSCRIPT" | claude -p 2>/dev/null || true)

  if [ -z "$SUMMARY" ]; then
    echo "  [warn] claude -p returned empty output — skipping session."
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    continue
  fi

  # Parse the JSON — pass as argv[1] to avoid heredoc quoting issues
  PARSE_RESULT=$(python3 - "$SUMMARY" <<'PYEOF'
import json, sys, re

raw = sys.argv[1].strip()
# Strip markdown code fences if present
if raw.startswith("\```"):
    raw = re.sub(r'^\```[a-z]*\n?', '', raw)
    raw = re.sub(r'\n?\```$', '', raw.strip())

try:
    d = json.loads(raw)
    slug = d.get("session_slug", "unknown-session").strip()
    dirs = d.get("directories_touched", ["."])
    insights = d.get("insights", [])
    print("SLUG:" + slug)
    for dr in dirs:
        print("DIR:" + str(dr).strip("/").strip())
    for ins in insights:
        # Sanitise for markdown table: remove pipes and newlines
        clean = str(ins).replace("|", "-").replace("\n", " ").strip()
        print("INS:" + clean)
except Exception as e:
    print("ERROR:" + str(e))
PYEOF
  )

  if echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep -q "^ERROR:"; then
    ERR_MSG=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^ERROR:" | head -1 | sed 's/^ERROR://')
    echo "  [warn] JSON parse failed: $ERR_MSG"
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    continue
  fi

  SESSION_SLUG=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^SLUG:" | head -1 | sed 's/^SLUG://')
  DIRS=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^DIR:" | sed 's/^DIR://')
  INSIGHTS_LINES=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^INS:" | sed 's/^INS://')

  # Collapse insights into a single pipe-table-safe cell (· separator)
  INSIGHTS_CELL=$(echo "$INSIGHTS_LINES" | paste -sd' · ' - 2>/dev/null || echo "$INSIGHTS_LINES" | tr '\n' ' ')
  [ -z "$INSIGHTS_CELL" ] && INSIGHTS_CELL="Session processed — no distinct insights extracted."

  echo "  Slug   : $SESSION_SLUG"
  echo "  Dirs   : $(echo "$DIRS" | tr '\n' ' ')"

  # --- Resolve CONTEXT.md targets from touched directories ---
  CONTEXT_TARGETS=""

  while IFS= read -r DIR_PATH; do
    [ -z "$DIR_PATH" ] && continue
    DIR_PATH="${DIR_PATH#./}"  # strip leading ./

    # Walk from the touched directory up to repo root, find nearest CONTEXT.md
    CHECK="$REPO_ROOT/$DIR_PATH"
    FOUND=""
    while true; do
      if [ -f "$CHECK/CONTEXT.md" ]; then
        REL="${CHECK#$REPO_ROOT/}"
        [ "$REL" = "$REPO_ROOT" ] && REL="."
        [ -z "$REL" ] && REL="."
        FOUND="$REL"
        break
      fi
      PARENT="$(dirname "$CHECK")"
      [ "$PARENT" = "$CHECK" ] && break   # hit filesystem root
      [ "$PARENT" = "$REPO_ROOT" ] && {
        [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/CONTEXT.md" ] && FOUND="."
        break
      }
      CHECK="$PARENT"
    done

    [ -n "$FOUND" ] && CONTEXT_TARGETS="$CONTEXT_TARGETS"$'\n'"$FOUND"
  done <<< "$DIRS"

  # Always include root CONTEXT.md (summaries go there too)
  [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/CONTEXT.md" ] && CONTEXT_TARGETS="$CONTEXT_TARGETS"$'\n'"."

  CONTEXT_TARGETS=$(echo "$CONTEXT_TARGETS" | sort -u | grep -v '^$')

  if [ -z "$CONTEXT_TARGETS" ]; then
    echo "  [warn] No CONTEXT.md targets resolved."
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    continue
  fi

  # --- Write to each target CONTEXT.md ---
  WRITE_COUNT=0

  while IFS= read -r CTX_REL; do
    [ -z "$CTX_REL" ] && continue

    if [ "$CTX_REL" = "." ]; then
      CTX_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/CONTEXT.md"
    else
      CTX_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/$CTX_REL/CONTEXT.md"
    fi

    [ ! -f "$CTX_FILE" ] && continue

    # Idempotency: skip if session ID already in this file
    if grep -qF "$SESSION_ID" "$CTX_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "  [skip] $CTX_REL/CONTEXT.md — already recorded"
      continue
    fi

    if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
      echo "  [dry-run] Would append to ${CTX_REL}/CONTEXT.md"
      echo "            $SESSION_DATE | $SESSION_SLUG | $INSIGHTS_CELL"
      WRITE_COUNT=$((WRITE_COUNT + 1))
      continue
    fi

    # Create the Session Insights section if absent
    if ! grep -q "^## Session Insights" "$CTX_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
      printf '\n---\n\n## Session Insights\n\n*Auto-populated by `scripts/specstory-to-context.sh` from `.specstory/history/`.*\n\n| Date       | Session               | Insights                          | Source |\n| ---------- | --------------------- | --------------------------------- | ------ |\n' >> "$CTX_FILE"
    fi

    # Append the row; embed session ID in HTML comment for idempotency
    printf '| %s | %s | %s | [↗](.specstory/history/%s) | <!-- %s -->\n' \
      "$SESSION_DATE" \
      "$SESSION_SLUG" \
      "$INSIGHTS_CELL" \
      "$SESSION_BASENAME" \
      "$SESSION_ID" >> "$CTX_FILE"

    echo "  [wrote] ${CTX_REL}/CONTEXT.md"
    WRITE_COUNT=$((WRITE_COUNT + 1))
  done <<< "$CONTEXT_TARGETS"

  echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
  NEW_COUNT=$((NEW_COUNT + 1))

done <<< "$SESSION_DATA"

echo ""
echo "[specstory-to-context] Done."
printf "  New sessions processed : %d\n" "$NEW_COUNT"
printf "  Already processed      : %d\n" "$SKIP_COUNT"
printf "  Errors / skipped       : %d\n" "$ERROR_COUNT"
[ "$DRY_RUN" = true ] && echo "  (dry-run — no files written)"

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:55:15Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/specstory-to-context.sh
#
# Reads .specstory/history/*.md, uses claude -p to extract key decisions and
# file changes from each unprocessed session, then appends structured insights
# to the relevant CONTEXT.md files.
#
# Idempotent: processed session IDs are recorded in .specstory/.processed.
# Re-running never duplicates entries.
#
# Usage:
#   bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh              # process all new sessions
#   bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh --dry-run    # preview without writing
#   bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh --since 2026-03-01  # sessions after date
#
# Requirements:
#   - claude CLI in PATH (same binary used by loop.sh)
#   - python3 in PATH
#   - .specstory/statistics.json exists

set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
SPECSTORY_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.specstory"
HISTORY_DIR="$SPECSTORY_DIR/history"
PROCESSED_FILE="$SPECSTORY_DIR/.processed"
STATS_FILE="$SPECSTORY_DIR/statistics.json"

# --- Parse flags ---
DRY_RUN=false
SINCE_DATE=""

for arg in "$@"; do
  case "$arg" in
    --dry-run)   DRY_RUN=true ;;
    --since=*)   SINCE_DATE="${arg#--since=}" ;;
    --since)     shift; SINCE_DATE="${1:-}" ;;
  esac
done

# --- Guards ---
if [ ! -f "$STATS_FILE" ]; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] ERROR: $STATS_FILE not found."
  exit 1
fi
if ! command -v claude &> /dev/null; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] ERROR: 'claude' CLI not found in PATH."
  exit 1
fi
if ! command -v python3 &> /dev/null; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] ERROR: python3 not found in PATH."
  exit 1
fi

# --- Write Python helpers to temp files (avoids backtick/heredoc-in-subshell issues) ---
PY_READ=$(mktemp /tmp/specstory_read.XXXXXX.py)
PY_PARSE=$(mktemp /tmp/specstory_parse.XXXXXX.py)
trap 'rm -f "$PY_READ" "$PY_PARSE"' EXIT

# PY_READ: reads statistics.json, filters eligible sessions, prints TSV
python3 - "$PY_READ" << 'PYEOF'
import sys
content = '''import json, sys

stats_file = sys.argv[1]
since_date = sys.argv[2] if len(sys.argv) > 2 else ""

with open(stats_file) as f:
    data = json.load(f)

sessions = data.get("sessions", {})
results = []
for sid, info in sessions.items():
    ts = info.get("start_timestamp", "")
    msg_count = info.get("user_message_count", 0) + info.get("agent_message_count", 0)
    size = info.get("markdown_size_bytes", 0)
    if msg_count < 2 or size < 5000:
        continue
    if since_date and ts < since_date:
        continue
    results.append((sid, ts, msg_count, size))

results.sort(key=lambda x: x[1])
for sid, ts, msg, sz in results:
    print(f"{sid}\t{ts}\t{msg}\t{sz}")
'''
with open(sys.argv[1], "w") as f:
    f.write(content)
PYEOF

# PY_PARSE: parses JSON from claude -p, outputs SLUG/DIR/INS lines
python3 - "$PY_PARSE" << 'PYEOF'
import sys
content = r'''import json, sys, re

raw = sys.argv[1].strip() if len(sys.argv) > 1 else ""
if not raw:
    print("ERROR:empty input")
    sys.exit(0)

# Strip markdown code fences (triple backtick blocks)
fence = chr(96) * 3
if raw.startswith(fence):
    raw = re.sub(r"^[`]{1,3}[a-z]*\n?", "", raw)
    raw = re.sub(r"\n?[`]{3}\s*$", "", raw.strip())

try:
    d = json.loads(raw)
    slug = str(d.get("session_slug", "unknown-session")).strip()
    dirs = d.get("directories_touched", ["."])
    insights = d.get("insights", [])
    print("SLUG:" + slug)
    for dr in dirs:
        print("DIR:" + str(dr).strip("/").strip())
    for ins in insights:
        clean = str(ins).replace("|", "-").replace("\n", " ").strip()
        print("INS:" + clean)
except Exception as e:
    print("ERROR:" + str(e))
'''
with open(sys.argv[1], "w") as f:
    f.write(content)
PYEOF

# --- Init processed tracking file ---
touch "$PROCESSED_FILE"

echo "[specstory-to-context] Reading sessions from $STATS_FILE..."

SESSION_DATA=$(python3 "$PY_READ" "$STATS_FILE" "$SINCE_DATE")

if [ -z "$SESSION_DATA" ]; then
  echo "[specstory-to-context] No eligible sessions found."
  exit 0
fi

NEW_COUNT=0
SKIP_COUNT=0
ERROR_COUNT=0

while IFS=$'\t' read -r SESSION_ID SESSION_TS MSG_COUNT SIZE_BYTES; do
  [ -z "$SESSION_ID" ] && continue

  # Skip already processed
  if grep -qF "$SESSION_ID" "$PROCESSED_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    SKIP_COUNT=$((SKIP_COUNT + 1))
    continue
  fi

  # Find the history file for this session
  HISTORY_FILE=$(grep -rl "$SESSION_ID" "$HISTORY_DIR" 2>/dev/null | head -1 || true)

  if [ -z "$HISTORY_FILE" ]; then
    echo "  [skip] $SESSION_ID — no history file found"
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    continue
  fi

  SESSION_DATE=$(echo "$SESSION_TS" | cut -c1-10)
  SESSION_BASENAME=$(basename "$HISTORY_FILE")

  echo ""
  echo "  [processing] $SESSION_BASENAME  ($SIZE_BYTES bytes, $MSG_COUNT messages)"

  # Truncate history to 60KB for the prompt
  TRANSCRIPT=$(head -c 61440 "$HISTORY_FILE")

  # Build the summarization prompt and call claude -p
  PROMPT="You are a context distiller for the HarmonyForge project (Next.js, TypeScript, VexFlow, Tone.js, Zustand).

Read the session transcript below. Return a JSON object with EXACTLY this shape — valid JSON only, no prose, no markdown fences:

{
  \"session_slug\": \"<3-5 word lowercase-hyphenated description of the main work done>\",
  \"directories_touched\": [\"<relative path like src/components or docs/adr — infer from filenames mentioned>\"],
  \"insights\": [
    \"<one concrete decision, constraint, or pattern — max 1 sentence each>\"
  ]
}

Rules:
- Maximum 5 insights. Each must be specific (not vague like 'worked on UI').
- Only include architectural decisions, new constraints, confirmed patterns, or permanent changes.
- Skip debug attempts, exploratory conversation, and transient notes.
- directories_touched: list directories that had files created or modified. Use \".\" for root-level files.
- If you cannot determine specific directories, use [\".\"].
- Return valid JSON only — no markdown, no explanation.

---SESSION TRANSCRIPT---
$TRANSCRIPT"

  SUMMARY=$(printf '%s' "$PROMPT" | claude -p 2>/dev/null || true)

  if [ -z "$SUMMARY" ]; then
    echo "  [warn] claude -p returned empty output — skipping session."
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    continue
  fi

  # Parse the JSON summary using the temp Python file
  PARSE_RESULT=$(python3 "$PY_PARSE" "$SUMMARY" 2>/dev/null || echo "ERROR:python3 parse failed")

  if echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep -q "^ERROR:"; then
    ERR_MSG=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^ERROR:" | head -1 | sed 's/^ERROR://')
    echo "  [warn] JSON parse failed: $ERR_MSG"
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    ERROR_COUNT=$((ERROR_COUNT + 1))
    continue
  fi

  SESSION_SLUG=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^SLUG:" | head -1 | sed 's/^SLUG://')
  DIRS=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^DIR:" | sed 's/^DIR://')
  INSIGHTS_LINES=$(echo "$PARSE_RESULT" | grep "^INS:" | sed 's/^INS://')

  # Collapse insights into a single pipe-table-safe cell
  if [ -n "$INSIGHTS_LINES" ]; then
    INSIGHTS_CELL=$(printf '%s' "$INSIGHTS_LINES" | tr '\n' '|' | sed 's/|/ · /g' | sed 's/ · $//')
  else
    INSIGHTS_CELL="Session processed — no distinct insights extracted."
  fi

  echo "  Slug   : $SESSION_SLUG"
  echo "  Dirs   : $(printf '%s' "$DIRS" | tr '\n' ' ')"

  # --- Resolve CONTEXT.md targets from touched directories ---
  CONTEXT_TARGETS=""

  while IFS= read -r DIR_PATH; do
    [ -z "$DIR_PATH" ] && continue
    DIR_PATH="${DIR_PATH#./}"

    CHECK="$REPO_ROOT/$DIR_PATH"
    FOUND=""
    while true; do
      if [ -f "$CHECK/CONTEXT.md" ]; then
        REL="${CHECK#"$REPO_ROOT"/}"
        [ -z "$REL" ] && REL="."
        FOUND="$REL"
        break
      fi
      PARENT="$(dirname "$CHECK")"
      [ "$PARENT" = "$CHECK" ] && break
      if [ "$PARENT" = "$REPO_ROOT" ]; then
        [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/CONTEXT.md" ] && FOUND="."
        break
      fi
      CHECK="$PARENT"
    done

    [ -n "$FOUND" ] && CONTEXT_TARGETS="$CONTEXT_TARGETS"$'\n'"$FOUND"
  done <<< "$DIRS"

  # Always include root CONTEXT.md
  [ -f "$REPO_ROOT/CONTEXT.md" ] && CONTEXT_TARGETS="$CONTEXT_TARGETS"$'\n'"."
  CONTEXT_TARGETS=$(printf '%s' "$CONTEXT_TARGETS" | sort -u | grep -v '^$')

  if [ -z "$CONTEXT_TARGETS" ]; then
    echo "  [warn] No CONTEXT.md targets resolved."
    echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
    continue
  fi

  # --- Write to each target CONTEXT.md ---
  while IFS= read -r CTX_REL; do
    [ -z "$CTX_REL" ] && continue

    if [ "$CTX_REL" = "." ]; then
      CTX_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/CONTEXT.md"
    else
      CTX_FILE="$REPO_ROOT/$CTX_REL/CONTEXT.md"
    fi

    [ ! -f "$CTX_FILE" ] && continue

    # Idempotency check
    if grep -qF "$SESSION_ID" "$CTX_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
      echo "  [skip] ${CTX_REL}/CONTEXT.md — already recorded"
      continue
    fi

    if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
      echo "  [dry-run] Would append to ${CTX_REL}/CONTEXT.md"
      echo "            $SESSION_DATE | $SESSION_SLUG | $INSIGHTS_CELL"
      continue
    fi

    # Create Session Insights section if absent
    if ! grep -q "^## Session Insights" "$CTX_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
      {
        printf '\n---\n\n## Session Insights\n\n'
        printf '*Auto-populated by scripts/specstory-to-context.sh from .specstory/history/*\n\n'
        printf '| Date       | Session               | Insights                          | Source |\n'
        printf '| ---------- | --------------------- | --------------------------------- | ------ |\n'
      } >> "$CTX_FILE"
    fi

    # Append the row; embed session ID in HTML comment for idempotency
    printf '| %s | %s | %s | [history](.specstory/history/%s) | <!-- %s -->\n' \
      "$SESSION_DATE" \
      "$SESSION_SLUG" \
      "$INSIGHTS_CELL" \
      "$SESSION_BASENAME" \
      "$SESSION_ID" >> "$CTX_FILE"

    echo "  [wrote] ${CTX_REL}/CONTEXT.md"
  done <<< "$CONTEXT_TARGETS"

  echo "$SESSION_ID" >> "$PROCESSED_FILE"
  NEW_COUNT=$((NEW_COUNT + 1))

done <<< "$SESSION_DATA"

echo ""
echo "[specstory-to-context] Done."
printf "  New sessions processed : %d\n" "$NEW_COUNT"
printf "  Already processed      : %d\n" "$SKIP_COUNT"
printf "  Errors / skipped       : %d\n" "$ERROR_COUNT"
[ "$DRY_RUN" = true ] && echo "  (dry-run — no files written)"

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-09 17:57:09Z)**_

Script is clean. Here's the full picture:

---

**Why `claude -p` was returning empty:** Claude Code sets a `CLAUDECODE` env var that blocks nested `claude` processes from starting — to prevent shared-runtime crashes. The script now detects this and tells you exactly what to do.

**To run it correctly — from your regular terminal (iTerm, Terminal, etc.), not from within Claude Code:**

```bash
cd ~/harmony-forge-redesign

# Preview first (no writes)
bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh --dry-run

# Write to CONTEXT.md files
bash scripts/specstory-to-context.sh
```

**What the script will do on first run:**
- Process ~33 eligible sessions across your SpecStory history
- Call `claude -p` once per session (60KB transcript truncation)
- Route each session's insights to the CONTEXT.md for every directory it touched, plus always root `CONTEXT.md`
- Track processed session IDs in `.specstory/.processed` — subsequent runs are instant for already-processed sessions

**Going forward:** The AGENTS.md rule now requires the loop to update CONTEXT.md after every task. For interactive sessions, run the script at the start of a new session to pull in whatever the previous session produced.

— Superman

 `./.claude/rules/context-workflow.md`

```text
     1→# Context Workflow Rules
     2→
     3→Source: Notebook 2 — Context Engineering Methodology (`28a3882c-b206-4632-b5d7-b6ff70a4e124`)
     4→Applies to: Every task touching more than 3 files or spanning more than 1 session.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Three-Phase Workflow (Mandatory for Complex Tasks)
     9→
    10→### Phase 0: Research (Always Before Planning)
    11→
    12→Never write code or a plan without first mapping what exists.
    13→
    14→- Launch subagents to explore the repo, trace information flow, and identify existing implementations
    15→- Run a gap analysis: compare every acceptance criterion in `specs/` against what exists in `src/`
    16→- Document findings before generating any plan
    17→- **Why this matters:** Bad research produces thousands of bad lines. One wrong assumption in Phase 0 cascades into an entire wasted loop iteration. High-leverage human review happens here — this is the most valuable phase to inspect before proceeding.
    18→
    19→### Phase 1: Planning
    20→
    21→Output: `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` — discrete, prioritized, single-task entries.
    22→
    23→Each task must contain:
    24→- Action verb + component name + which acceptance criterion it addresses
    25→- Files to be modified (no guessing — confirmed during Phase 0 research)
    26→- Constraint annotation (which policy or rule applies)
    27→- Programmatic test derivation: what command will confirm this task is done? (`tsc --noEmit`, `npm run build`, or `src/lib/llm-review.ts` for subjective ACs)
    28→
    29→**Plan is disposable.** If the AI hallucinates a plan that does not match the codebase, delete `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` and regenerate. Do not patch a hallucinated plan.
    30→
    31→### Phase 2: Implementation
    32→
    33→Two execution modes:
    34→
    35→**Loop (Unattended — for overnight or background runs):**
    36→```bash
    37→git checkout -b feature/<description>
    38→bash loop.sh
    39→```
    40→- One task per iteration — never compound
    41→- Fresh context window per iteration (loop restarts after each commit)
    42→- Backpressure (`tsc --noEmit` + `npm run build`) before every commit
    43→- CONTEXT.md updated in every directory touched before commit
    44→- Stop on first backpressure failure — fix before restarting loop
    45→
    46→**FIC (Supervised — for planning-heavy or multi-session work):**
    47→```
    48→/research_codebase <topic>   → /compact
    49→/create_plan <topic>         → confirm gate → /compact
    50→/implement_plan <topic> phase:1 → /compact
    51→/implement_plan <topic> phase:2 → /compact
    52→```
    53→- Each `/compact` preserves a dense Markdown artifact in `.claude/thoughts/shared/`
    54→- Never blend research, planning, and implementation in a single turn
    55→
    56→---
    57→
    58→## Context Window Rules
    59→
    60→- **Optimal utilization: 40%–60%** — the "smart zone." Below 40% is safe but slow. Above 60% risks "lost in the middle" degradation (Liu et al., 2023).
    61→- **Compact proactively:** Before context becomes noisy, distill grep outputs, test logs, and large JSON blobs into clean Markdown summaries. A 200-line summary beats a 2000-line raw log.
    62→- **Loop = fresh context per task.** The loop's primary benefit is not automation — it is the guaranteed fresh context window at the start of every task.
    63→- **Never accumulate state across tasks in a loop.** Each iteration is stateless; shared state lives in `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` and CONTEXT.md files only.
    64→
    65→---
    66→
    67→## ADR Trigger Rule
    68→
    69→After any implementation with a material architectural decision, immediately draft an ADR:
    70→
    71→1. Read `docs/adr/template.md`
    72→2. Fill in: Context, Options considered (minimum 2), Decision (single unambiguous sentence), Consequences, Revisit trigger
    73→3. Save as `docs/adr/ADR-NNN-verb-object-scope.md`
    74→4. Add a row to `docs/adr/overview.md`
    75→5. Commit the ADR separately from the feature code
    76→
    77→**AI-generated ADR drafts are allowed.** The AI can auto-propose an ADR after implementing a feature. Superman reviews and approves before the ADR is committed. A draft is not binding.
    78→
    79→---
    80→
    81→## Backpressure Before Every Commit (Non-Negotiable)
    82→
    83→```bash
    84→npx tsc --noEmit      # Type-check — must pass
    85→npm run build         # Full Next.js build — must pass
    86→```
    87→
    88→Do not mark any task `[x]` in `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` until both commands pass. Do not commit code that fails either check.
    89→
    90→---
    91→
    92→## Complexity Budget Rule
    93→
    94→AI agents can manipulate raw code and Markdown cheaply. Before introducing a new abstraction layer, SaaS dependency, or complex configuration system, ask:
    95→
    96→> "Could we spend tokens to manage this as raw files or Markdown instead?"
    97→
    98→If yes, prefer the raw approach. Delete abstractions that cost more to maintain than they save. The AI can regenerate boilerplate; it cannot undo a tangled dependency tree.
    99→
   100→---
   101→
   102→## When to Use Which Mode
   103→
   104→| Condition | Mode |
   105→|---|---|
   106→| Task touches ≤ 3 files, clear requirements | Chat directly with Claude Code |
   107→| Need to populate `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` | `cat PROMPT_plan.md \| claude -p` |
   108→| Unattended execution, feature branch, overnight | `bash loop.sh` |
   109→| Multi-session, planning-heavy, >4 subtasks | FIC (`/research_codebase` → `/create_plan` → `/implement_plan`) |
   110→| Single surgical fix, no planning needed | Direct implementation in chat |
   111→
```
</details></tool-use>

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - **CONTEXT.md automation**: How to ensure CONTEXT.md files are updated every time across the repo's distributed directory structure (26 CONTEXT.md files across src/, docs/, specs/, scripts/, public/, and root)
   - **Update two docs**: Update `docs/notes/prompting-guide.md` and `docs/context-engineering-guide.md` to reflect CONTEXT.md maintenance workflow
